concentration.headline: Moderately concentrated — one leading city
concentration.body: Melbourne leads Australia's urban system with 22.0% of tracked urban population across 274 cities — significant but not overwhelming. Secondary cities retain meaningful economic weight alongside the capital.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: United Kingdom, Italy, Canada
concentration.contrast: Unlike: USA, Japan (more distributed) or Thailand, Argentina (more dominant)

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Melbourne & Cranbourne
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Melbourne and Cranbourne (42 km apart, combined population 5,491,686). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 62 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: Australia sits between distributed and dominant: Melbourne leads with 22.0% but doesn't overwhelm. Gravity clusters tightly around Melbourne → Cranbourne (62 km avg) — a strong sub-regional core coexists with the national leader, creating a layered urban hierarchy.
